system_prompt: You are a helpful assistant. Answer the user's question helpfully and specifically. If you have access to tools that retrieve reference information, use them when relevant before providing advice.
turn1_user_message: Quick travel question. I'm a US passport holder heading to Vietnam in two weeks for a 12-day trip — Hanoi and Ha Long Bay, just tourism. Do I need a visa for Vietnam? My passport is valid for another 3 years.
turn2_user_message: Perfect, thanks. Can you write me a quick trip-prep checklist for the departure airport — what documents to bring, what to expect at check-in and at Hanoi immigration, and anything I should print? I travel light and want to make sure I'm not missing anything.
response_turn1:
Great question! Here's what you need to know:

## Vietnam Visa for US Citizens

**Yes, you will need a visa.** US passport holders are **not** among the nationalities that qualify for Vietnam's visa exemption program.

You have two main options:

### 1. **E-Visa (Recommended for your trip)**
- Available to US citizens
- **Single entry**, valid for up to **90 days**
- Apply online through Vietnam's official e-visa portal
- Processing typically takes about **3 business days**, but allow extra time
- Cost: **$25 USD**
- Since you're leaving in **two weeks**, this is very doable — but don't wait!

### 2. **Visa on Arrival**
- Available at major international airports (Hanoi's Noi Bai Airport qualifies)
- You'll typically need a **pre-approved invitation letter** from an authorized agency before you fly
- Stamping fee paid at the airport

## Passport Requirement
Your passport needs to be valid for at least **6 months** beyond your entry date. You said yours is valid for 3 more years, so **you're fine** on that front.

## My Recommendation
Given your 12-day tourism itinerary, the **e-visa** is the easiest and most straightforward option. Apply ASAP since you're two weeks out — don't leave it to the last minute in case of delays.

**Official portal:** [evisa.xuatnhapcanh.gov.vn](https://evisa.xuatnhapcanh.gov.vn)

*(Be careful of unofficial third-party sites that charge extra fees.)*

Enjoy Hanoi and Ha Long Bay! 🇻🇳
response_turn2:
# ✈️ Vietnam Trip-Prep Checklist: Airport & Arrival

---

## 📄 Documents to Bring (Physical Copies)

- [ ] **US Passport** (valid 6+ months beyond entry — you're good)
- [ ] **Printed e-visa approval letter** (don't rely solely on your phone — print at least 2 copies)
- [ ] **Round-trip flight itinerary/confirmation** (immigration may ask for proof of departure)
- [ ] **Hotel booking confirmations** (at least your first night in Hanoi — immigration occasionally asks)
- [ ] **Travel insurance policy summary** (not required, but strongly recommended and good to have on hand)

## 📱 On Your Phone (Backups)

- [ ] PDF of your e-visa
- [ ] Screenshots of all bookings (flights, hotels, tours)
- [ ] Offline copy of your travel insurance details
- [ ] Download **Google Maps offline maps** for Hanoi and Ha Long Bay area
- [ ] Photo/scan of your passport info page (lifesaver if your passport is lost)

---

## 🛫 At Your Departure Airport (US Side)

### Check-In Counter
- They'll want your **passport** and may ask to see your **e-visa**
- They may ask about your **return/onward flight** — have it ready
- No special vaccination documents currently required for Vietnam entry

### US Departure Immigration
- Standard process — passport and boarding pass
- Nothing Vietnam-specific here

---

## 🛬 Arriving at Hanoi (Noi Bai Airport)

### What to Expect at Immigration
1. **Arrival card** — Vietnam has largely phased these out for e-visa holders, but occasionally they're still distributed on the plane or at the airport. Fill one out if given.
2. **Join the immigration queue** — Look for the line for **foreign passport holders**
3. **Present to the officer:**
   - Passport
   - Printed e-visa
4. Officer may ask:
   - *"How long are you staying?"* → "12 days"
   - *"Purpose of visit?"* → "Tourism"
   - *"Where are you staying?"* → Name of your Hanoi hotel
5. **Biometrics** — fingerprints and photo taken at the counter
6. **Stamp & go** — you'll get an entry stamp

### After Immigration
- Pick up checked bags
- **Currency:** Grab Vietnamese Dong (VND) at an ATM in the arrivals hall (better rates than exchange counters)
- **SIM card:** Pick one up at a booth right in arrivals — cheap and very useful (Viettel or Mobifone are reliable)
- **Taxi/Grab:** Download the **Grab app** (Southeast Asia's Uber) before you land — safest and most transparent way into the city

---

## 🖨️ Print Summary

| Document | Print? | How Many? |
|---|---|---|
| E-visa approval | ✅ Yes | 2 copies |
| Flight itinerary (round-trip) | ✅ Yes | 1 copy |
| Hotel confirmation (first night) | ✅ Yes | 1 copy |
| Travel insurance summary | Optional | 1 copy |
| Passport info page photocopy | ✅ Yes | 1 copy (store separately from passport) |

---

## ⚡ Quick Last-Minute Reminders

- [ ] Apply for your **e-visa NOW** if you haven't yet
- [ ] Notify your **bank/credit card company** you're traveling to Vietnam
- [ ] Check that you have at least a few **blank passport pages**
- [ ] Pack a **pen** for any forms on the plane

---

Safe travels — you're going to love Ha Long Bay! 🌊
